notes: Capstone integration test only, closes GitHub issue #15's implementation work (issue not closed by this commit).

Shared hivemind.proto gRPC service (6 RPCs) + EdgeType enum + generated Go/Python stubs, issue #16 Epic Phase 3. Contract-definition-only, no server/client wiring yet. Fix cycle fixed Neighbor.weight float->uint32 type mismatch; docs/LLD/rpc.md updated to disclose Split intentionally excluded from the 6-RPC surface.

engine/rpc/server.go: Go-side handlers for 5 of 6 HiveMind gRPC RPCs, delegating to real engine/catalog, engine/graph, engine/btree primitives, no mocks; ProposeSplit left as Unimplemented stub for 3.2.3. Caught and fixed a real EdgeType Go-iota-vs-proto-wire-value mismatch bug via explicit protoEdgeTypeToGraph/graphEdgeTypeToProto conversion functions. Non-blocking FileId=0 error-code-mapping gap disclosed, deferred to milestone #10 (Phase 4.5).

engine/split/proposer_grpc.go: real gRPC-backed GRPCSplitProposer client implementation of split.SplitProposer, calling hivemindv1.HiveMindClient.ProposeSplit with optional per-call timeout and clean non-panicking error wrapping. proposer_mock.go and engine/rpc/server.go untouched, purely additive. Verified via genuine bufconn wire round-trip test (happy_path, empty_response, server_error, wall-clock-verified timeout). Clean PASS, no bugs found.

engine/rpc/interceptor.go: per-call gRPC server LatencyInterceptor (wall-clock duration + payload-size cost proxy) emitting RPCMetric to a pluggable Recorder, default slog-based. Additive only, not yet wired into a production server. Folded-in fix: GetFile/ReadPartial now return InvalidArgument (not Internal) for FileId=0, resolving task-3.2.2 non-blocking finding, independently re-verified.

engine/rpc/integration_test.go: capstone TestRPCIntegration wiring LatencyInterceptor into a real grpc.Server over a real net.Listener, driving PutSegment/GetFile/ReadPartial/GraphNeighbors/SearchCandidates/ProposeSplit against real on-disk backing state. Closes out issue #16's 3.2.x gRPC sequence. Test-only, no production code touched. Carries forward disclosed scope caveat: ProposeSplit asserted as codes.Unimplemented (Go-only), not a real cross-language Go<->Python round trip as issue #16's literal text specifies -- deferred to issue #18, open item pending user sign-off before issue #16 GitHub closure. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS.

agents/ingestion/normalize_pdf.py: pymupdf-based PDF-to-text normalizer emitting [[PAGE n LEN=k]]...[[/PAGE n]] page-boundary markers for downstream ingestion/dispatch. Fix cycle closed a medium-severity content-truncation bug (F1: content-scanning marker collision) by switching to length-prefixed framing. Non-blocking low-severity findings F3 (LEN trust boundary) and F4 (no Unicode round-trip test) deferred to milestone #10 (Phase 4.5, issues #38-42).

agents/ingestion/normalize_email.py: stdlib email.parser-based Enron-format email normalizer producing EmailFields (sender/subject/thread/body). Thread-id derivation is a disclosed tiered fallback (In-Reply-To -> References root -> normalized-subject key). Multi-prefix subject stripping, multipart, display-name-From, and quoted-printable body handling independently verified correct, but not exercised by the shipped test fixtures -- non-blocking test-coverage gap forward-referenced to milestone #10 (Phase 4.5, issues #38-42). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS.

agents/ingestion/normalize_ticket.py: JSON/CSV support-ticket normalizer emitting labeled scalar fields plus length-prefixed [[COMMENT n LEN=k]]...[[/COMMENT n]] comment-block markers, mirroring normalize_pdf.py's [[PAGE n LEN=k]] pattern. Fix cycle closed a medium-severity recurrence of the 3.3.1 marker-collision bug class (F1: reverted to pre-fix non-length-prefixed comment markers) by adopting the same length-prefixed framing. Minor non-blocking docstring-overclaim finding noted inline (not separately tracked). Suggests (non-mandatory) a shared marker-framing utility for 3.3.4 if a third marker format is needed.

agents/ingestion/rawdoc.py (RawDocument frozen dataclass) + agents/ingestion/dispatch.py (dispatch() + per-source-type convenience wrappers): common hand-off record and single entry point routing to the three already-verified normalizers (normalize_pdf, normalize_email, normalize_ticket) by source_type. No existing normalizer files modified. Final subtask of issue #17; all 4 subtasks (3.3.1-3.3.4) now verified. Non-blocking minor finding: dispatch_pdf recomputes page_count via a redundant second iter_pages() pass. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, no fix cycle.

agents/llm/client.py (LLMClient ABC + LLMError) + agents/llm/ollama_client.py (OllamaClient + OllamaClientError): provider-agnostic single-shot completion interface and a local-Ollama-backed implementation calling /api/generate, default model llama3.1:8b. First real code in previously scaffold-only agents/llm/. Purely additive. Tested via httpx.MockTransport, no real network. First subtask (1/6) of issue #18 (segmentation agent, milestone #5); 5 subtasks remain. Non-blocking finding: no test for non-string 'response' value error path. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, no fix cycle.
shortlist() bounds SearchCandidates pool then re-ranks locally via Okapi BM25; new GrpcSearchCandidatesClient; non-blocking fabricated-LLD-quote docstring finding.

segment() builds segmentation prompt from RawDocument + shortlist, calls LLMClient, parses raw completion into validated SegmentResult (topic_action, target_topic, new_topic_path, content_markdown, entities, related_topics); new SegmentError/SegmentParseError hierarchy kept distinct from llm.client.LLMError; non-blocking findings F1 (markdown-code-fence JSON rejected, forward to 3.4.4/3.4.6) and F2 (target_topic not validated against catalog, forward to 3.4.4).

PutEdge/PutEntity/LookupEntity RPCs: user-authorized new scope surfaced mid-verification of issue #18 3.4.4 (F3), not a numbered GitHub subtask itself. PutEdge appends raw occurrences to graph.EdgeLog, leaving weight-summing/dedup to the existing Compact pass; PutEntity/LookupEntity implement entity.idx as a dedicated btree.Tree kept separate from the path index. Unblocks task-3.4.4b (agents/ingestion/wiring.py Python rewiring, not yet done). Non-blocking finding F5: shipped weight-sum test uses uniform weights.

Consolidated closure of issue #18 subtask 3.4.4: agents/ingestion/wiring.py SegmentWiringClient is now fully real-RPC-backed for PutSegment (pre-existing) and, via 3.4.4a engine RPCs + 3.4.4b GrpcEntityEdgeClient rewiring, for entity indexing (PutEntity/LookupEntity) and edge creation (PutEdge, ENTITY_COOCCUR/LLM_ASSERTED). See .cdr/commits/task-3.4.4.md. Non-blocking findings F4/F5/F6 carried forward.

propose_split() implements the Python gRPC-callee-side business logic for ProposeSplit: deterministic marker-based SectionRange resolution (no gaps/overlaps by construction), code-fence-stripping JSON parsing, LLMError propagation. Go-side client (engine/split/proposer_grpc.go) already done in task-3.2.3; zero Go/proto files touched here, independently confirmed via git diff. Non-blocking findings F2 (UTF-8 byte-offset conversion untested by non-ASCII fixtures) and F3 (substring-marker near-miss untested). Fifth of 6 subtasks toward issue #18; 1 subtask remains (3.4.6).

Fixture suite (test_segment_fixtures.py + testdata/notes_corpus/) with the repo's first end-to-end shortlist()->segment()->execute_segment() composition test; optional live-Ollama smoke test (test_segment_live.py, auto-skipped without a reachable local Ollama server); closes forwarded finding F1 via new shared agents/ingestion/_json_fences.strip_code_fences helper (also adopted by propose_split.py). Final (6th) subtask of issue #18 (segmentation agent, milestone #5, Phase 3) -- all 6 subtasks now implemented, verified, and committed locally, not pushed/closed. Non-blocking finding: shared fence helper doesn't handle multi-fence/prose-wrapped responses (inherited pre-existing limitation).

data/load_bitext.py + data/load_enron.py: dataset loaders mapping a real downloaded 30-row Bitext HF sample and a disclosed hand-authored Enron-format fixture sample onto agents/ingestion's existing normalize_ticket/normalize_email normalizer inputs. Both lazily import agents/ingestion (CWD/venv-independent). First of 2 subtasks toward issue #19; 3.5.2 (end-to-end smoke run) needs a real non-fixture Enron sample, flagged as hivemind-issue19-3.5.2-need-real-enron-sample.

agents/ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py + engine/cmd/smokeserver/main.go + data/fixtures/enron_real_sample/ (genuine real Enron corpus sample streamed from the official CMU/FERC archive, disclosed via PROVENANCE.md). First fully end-to-end pipeline test in the repo: real loaders -> normalize -> shortlist -> segment [real local Ollama llama3.1:8b] -> wiring.execute_segment [real PutSegment/PutEdge over real gRPC to a real standalone smokeserver subprocess]. F4 (PutSegment CREATE never sets PathHash) independently re-confirmed as requiring a proto/wire-contract change, not fixed here (documented blocker, not a skipped narrow fix). New non-blocking finding F7 (real LLM output can contain malformed JSON distinct from closed F1). FINAL (2nd of 2) subtask of issue #19 -- fully implemented, verified, and committed locally, not pushed/closed. See .cdr/commits/task-issue-19-summary.md.

agents/llm/openrouter_client.py: OpenRouterClient(LLMClient), GPT-4o-mini default, bearer API-key auth (OPENROUTER_API_KEY env or explicit kwarg), injectable httpx transport, fail-loud OpenRouterClientError(LLMError). 1st of 3 subtasks toward issue #20 (milestone #6, Phase 4: Query pipeline). Additive-only; no existing file modified.

agents/llm/gemini_client.py: GeminiClient(LLMClient) via direct REST (no SDK dep), GEMINI_API_KEY env or explicit kwarg, same shape as OpenRouterClient. 2nd of 3 subtasks toward issue #20. Non-blocking security note: API key sent as ?key= query param (Gemini REST convention, not a defect here).

agents/llm/factory.py::create_llm_client -- config-driven dispatch to Ollama/OpenRouter/Gemini via explicit arg or LLM_PROVIDER env var. Final (3rd) subtask of issue #20 (milestone #6, Phase 4: Query pipeline) -- all 3 subtasks now implemented, verified, and committed locally, not pushed/closed. Non-blocking comment: agents/ingestion/segment.py still constructs OllamaClient directly rather than via the factory, left as a future-consistency follow-up.

Real term-overlap ranking for SearchCandidates over the btree.PrefixScan pool; backward-compatible no-op for the only current caller (shortlist.py, query=""). Non-blocking design limitation: candidate pool restricted to paths prefix-matching the query's first whitespace token; must be addressed before issue #23's query-time topic selector is built on top of this RPC. See .cdr/commits/task-issue-21-summary.md and .cdr/memory/pending.md.

agents/query/intent_refiner.py refine_intent(): LLMClient-DI'd prompt-then-parse-JSON intent refiner, IntentRefinerResult{refined_intent, entities, query_type}. query_type taxonomy (factual_lookup/broad_exploratory) self-authored (LLD does not specify value names), closed 2-value Literal -- extending later touches intent_refiner.py itself, not just fixtures. Reuses agents.ingestion._json_fences.strip_code_fences (private cross-package import, non-blocking maintainability smell). 1st of 2 subtasks toward issue #22.

agents/query/test_intent_refiner_types.py: 6 additional non-overlapping query_type fixtures, test-only, zero production changes. Verifier flagged forward: refine_intent() has zero classification logic, so these tests prove JSON-parsing fidelity across fixtures, not real classification accuracy against unmocked LLM output -- relevant to whoever builds/verifies issue #23's topic_selector.py. _FakeLLMClient duplicated near-verbatim across test_intent_refiner.py and test_intent_refiner_types.py (non-blocking). 2nd/final subtask toward issue #22.

agents/query/topic_selector.py select_top_k()/TopicCandidate/DEFAULT_K=3: deterministic top-k selection over a SearchCandidates-shaped candidate pool, stable tie-break via (-score, original_index). No GrpcSearchCandidatesClient-style adapter exists yet in agents/query/ (unlike ingestion/shortlist.py precedent); SearchCandidatesFn is a typed injection point only, real wiring deferred to issue #25.

agents/query/topic_selector.py is_insufficient_alone()/expand_insufficient_topics()/GraphNeighbor/GraphNeighborsFn: ratio-relative-to-top insufficiency heuristic driving conditional 0-2 hop GraphNeighbors expansion (DEFAULT_EXPANSION_HOPS=2), range validated against engine/rpc/server.go. Docstring invariant (top topic never flagged for ratio<=1) is false for negative TopicCandidate.score; untested edge case, low real-world impact.

agents/query/topic_selector.py combine_and_cap(): dedups selected topics + expansion neighbors and enforces the system-wide k+2k hard cap with deterministic order-preserving truncation. Verified correct for k in {0,1,2,5,10} manually; only k=3 pinned down by a committed automated test (latent test-coverage gap, non-blocking).

agents/query/test_topic_selector_integration.py: 3 end-to-end scenarios composing the real select_top_k -> expand_insufficient_topics -> combine_and_cap pipeline with only GraphNeighborsFn mocked, exceeding the issue test-spec literal single-scenario wording. Test-only; topic_selector.py itself untouched. Closes out issue #23 (milestone #6, Phase 4 query pipeline).

agents/query/synthesizer.py: synthesize_answer(), SynthesizerResult, unknown_citations(). Prompt assembly from refined intent + selected markdown (## File: <path> headers), LLM JSON response parsed into answer prose with inline [<path>] citations plus a flat citations list. Follows intent_refiner.py conventions (TYPE_CHECKING-only DI, prompt-then-parse-JSON, frozen dataclass, SynthesizerError/SynthesizerParseError). unknown_citations() built as the 4.5.2 hallucination-detection building block. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/026-verification); non-blocking: citations dedup not code-enforced (F-4.5.1-1), exact-match citation semantics with no path normalization (F-4.5.1-2).

agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py: dedicated end-to-end test exercising synthesize_answer() with a fixture LLM response containing one valid + one hallucinated citation (both orderings), asserting exact-value detection via unknown_citations() and that flagging is additive (raw citations list unchanged). Test-only; synthesizer.py confirmed byte-for-byte unchanged via diff. Closes out issue #24 (milestone #6, Phase 4 query pipeline -- last open issue). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/028-verification); non-blocking: 3/4 tests substantially duplicate 4.5.1 test_synthesizer.py scenario (F-4.5.2-1), uncommitted adversarial edge-case coverage (F-4.5.2-2), prompt-injection attempt disclosed during verification (F-4.5.2-3).

agents/query/pipeline.py: run_query_pipeline() chains refine_intent -> select_top_k -> expand_insufficient_topics -> combine_and_cap -> get_file -> synthesize_answer, with search_candidates/graph_neighbors/get_file injected as callables (DI seam). Exposed via api/routes/query.go's POST /query route, registered in api/main.go behind a QueryPipeline Go interface; api/main.go wires a notImplementedPipeline stand-in since no real gRPC/HTTP client wiring exists yet. All call-site signatures verified byte-for-byte against real upstream functions. 1st of 2 subtasks under issue #25 (milestone #6, Phase 4 query pipeline). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/031-verification); non-blocking: /query returns 500 for every real request today pending real wiring (F-4.6.1-1), GetFileFn's (path, content) shape has no GetFileResponse proto counterpart (F-4.6.1-2).

agents/query/test_query_e2e.py: end-to-end test seeding a real 3-file on-disk corpus, running run_query_pipeline() for real (only LLMClient + search_candidates/graph_neighbors/get_file DI-seam callables faked, per 4.6.1's disclosed DI seam); asserts a valid citation resolves to a real seeded file and a hallucinated citation is correctly flagged. No production code modified (confirmed 0-diff). Closes out issue #25 (milestone #6, Phase 4 'Query pipeline' -- last open issue; Phase 4 functionally complete pending separate milestone-close step). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/033-verification); non-blocking: expand_insufficient_topics branch never exercised against the real seeded corpus in combination with citation resolution (F-4.6.2-1).

engine/graph/compact_test.go: TestCompactNormalTruncateThenAppendThenCompactAgain, exact-named regression test pinning wal.WriteSegmentFloor's existing fix (commit ed57468, predates issue #49) for sidecar-staleness/data-loss from WAL segment-number reuse after node truncate+recreate during Compact. No production code changed -- investigation confirmed the fix already shipped; verifier independently re-traced it against the issue's exact failure mode. Part of issue #49 (milestone #10, Phase 4.5 storage-engine technical debt). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/036-verification).

engine/graph/edgelog.go (nodeLocksMu/nodeLocks/LockNode) + engine/graph/compact.go (heldNodeLocks/releaseHeldLocks): new per-node *sync.Mutex mechanism closing a genuine lock-ordering race between Compact's ReadNodeAfter->TruncateNode sequence and a concurrent AppendEdge for the same node, which could silently drop the concurrent edge. Scoped per node, not global. New compactNodeLockedHook test seam (matching repo's crabRetryHook/atomicCommitHook idiom) + TestCompactConcurrentAppendNotLost prove it under -race. Part of issue #49 (milestone #10, Phase 4.5 storage-engine technical debt). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/038-verification); non-blocking: multi-node simultaneous lock-holding undocumented/untested (deadlock-safety of single-node case independently stress-tested and confirmed sound).

engine/graph/csr_test.go: TestLoadCSRRejectsUnknownEdgeType, exact-named regression test pinning decodeCSREdge's existing ValidEdgeType guard (added subtask 3.1.4, already matching edge_append.go's decodeEdge convention). Writes valid graph via WriteCSR, hand-patches on-disk edge Type byte to an out-of-range value and recomputes payload CRC32 (since WriteCSR itself refuses to persist an invalid type), isolating the assertion from the CRC-mismatch path. No production code changed. Part of issue #49 (milestone #10, Phase 4.5 storage-engine technical debt). Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/040-verification), independently mutation-tested by verifier to confirm non-vacuous; non-blocking: fixture only patches the first (edge-index-0) encoded edge, minor coverage gap for other edge indices.

Replaces informal ls-then-pick-next-number run-directory allocation convention (documented only in cdr-runtime skill markdown) with .cdr/tools/reserve-run-dir.sh, an mkdir+EEXIST atomic reservation loop, plus .cdr/tools/test-reserve-run-dir.sh verifying 20+ concurrent allocations produce zero collisions. Fixes collisions observed at runs 132-verification, 138-verification, and the 065/087-verification duplicate-verification split documented in 140-verification/verification.json.

LLD-sync pass (issue #58, 4.5.19.2): regenerated drifted sections of docs/LLD/ingestion-agent.md (Status, Segmentation-agent LLM-provider claim, What-the-Go-engine-does-with-each-segment RPC surface, Interactions) and docs/LLD/query-agent.md (Status, new Pipeline-wiring-&-real-gRPC-surface section covering pipeline.py/wiring.py/server.py, Interactions) to reflect the 26+ ingestion and 18+ query commits landed since the 699105b bootstrap doc-gen. Left query-agent.md's Known-risks SearchCandidates section untouched (owned by sibling subtask 4.5.19.4, commit 68c3c5c). Stamped last_synced_commit=68c3c5c4e504c2be7502959d78d0a3105b8cfeb5 (HEAD at sync time) in both docs' front-matter.

agents/eval/datasets.py: common load_dataset(name, *, limit=None)/available_datasets() interface over task-3.5.1's Bitext/Enron loaders (data/load_bitext.py, data/load_enron.py), reusing ingestion.rawdoc.RawDocument as the shared record shape so all three future benchmark arms (HiveMind, vector-RAG baseline, GraphRAG-style baseline) see an identical corpus per docs/LLD/eval.md. agents/eval/test_dataset_interface.py: 6 tests. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/10001-verification); non-blocking: sys.path mutation copied from test-only precedent, untested limit=0 boundary.

Ollama-only (never OpenRouter/Gemini) LLM-authored synthetic policy/manual PDF corpus generator (data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py) + topics.yaml seed + manifest.json for subtask 5.1.3's ground-truth derivation. PASS_WITH_COMMENTS; one non-blocking finding logged in regression.jsonl (render_pdf overflow dead-code).

Expanded data/synthetic_corpus/topics.yaml 10->32 topics (within ~30-50 LLD target); regenerated full synthetic PDF corpus + manifest.json via unchanged Ollama-only data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py. agents/eval/ground_truth.py auto-derives ground truth (primary/cross_reference relevance labels) purely structurally from manifest.json, zero LLM calls, zero hand-authored judgments; derive_query_set() generates one deterministic query per topic. Committed data/synthetic_corpus/ground_truth.json as the label-file artifact satisfying docs/LLD/eval.md's recall/precision query-label requirement. Final subtask of issue #26. PASS_WITH_COMMENTS; two non-blocking findings logged in regression.jsonl (spot-check hallucination-density undercount; unreproducible protobuf-mismatch claim due to environment drift, no actual regression).

Fixed-size-chunk classic vector-RAG baseline: word-boundary-snapped sliding-window chunker tuned via real grid-search sweep (select_chunk_config), local OllamaEmbeddingClient (nomic-embed-text via /api/embed), cosine-similarity VectorRagIndex.retrieve_documents. 100% local (Ollama only). Two non-blocking findings logged (see task-5.2.1 index entry / regression.jsonl).

Toggleable LLM-listwise reranking stage on top of the 5.2.1 vector-RAG baseline (local llama3.1:8b via existing LLMClient, no new dependency); real precision@1 improvement 0.0 -> 1.0 on fixture, reproduced 8/8 by verifier.

Third and final retrieval arm of the milestone #7 benchmark suite: entity-graph (GraphRAG-lite) baseline. Real LLM-based entity extraction (llama3.1:8b via existing LLMClient), stdlib-only co-occurrence EntityGraph (no networkx), capped-hop decayed BFS traversal (HOP_DECAY ** hop) as the disclosed mitigation for HLD's 'graph traversal context blow-up' risk. Output shape matches vector_rag.retrieve_documents exactly. Verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (.cdr/runs/2026-07-11/10016-verification); one non-blocking finding logged in regression.jsonl (unguarded substring-containment entity-match fallback, benchmark-fairness risk at real-corpus scale).

generate_final_answer() in agents/eval/pipeline.py is the sole final-answer LLM call path for all three benchmark arms (HiveMind, vector-RAG, GraphRAG-lite); verbatim reuse of query.synthesizer.synthesize_answer, mutation-tested to confirm the identical-call-path guarantee is genuinely enforced.

Canonical agents/eval/metrics.py: recall_at_k/precision_at_k consolidated out of vector_rag.py (5.2.1) and vector_rag_rerank.py (5.2.2) re-export shims, plus new relevant_doc_id_set/score_query/score_arm helpers over ground_truth.RelevantDoc/QueryLabel for uniform scoring of all three benchmark arms. First subtask of issue #28 (5.3.1/4); PASS_WITH_COMMENTS with two low-severity non-blocking findings in regression.jsonl.

agents/eval/cost_latency.py: StageRecord/aggregate_by_stage/rollup_cost_per_1000_queries per-arm per-stage latency + $/1000-query cost rollup for issue #28 milestone #7 (5.3.3 of 4). PASS_WITH_COMMENTS; F1 forward blocker for 5.3.4 (no OpenRouter/Gemini cost_usd or pricing table exists, ValueError on first non-Ollama record) tracked in regression.jsonl; F2 not yet wired into harness (expected, 5.3.4 integration point).
